//! Whether or not this catalog should search a specific type with the standard priority
DUCKDB_API virtual CatalogLookupBehavior CatalogTypeLookupRule(CatalogType type) const {
+	//! Connect this client to a remote-style AttachedDatabase. Subsequent non-control SQL routes via
+	//! Catalog::GetConnectFunctionName. Use DisconnectFromCatalog() to revert to LOCAL.
+	//! Clear any active CONNECT; subsequent SQL goes through the normal DuckDB pipeline.
+	//! True iff a CONNECT is currently active (even if the target was detached out from under us).
+	//! Resolve the currently-connected AttachedDatabase. Returns nullptr if not connected or if the
+	//! target has been detached out from under us (in that case IsConnected() is still true — call it
+	//! directly to disambiguate "never connected" from "was connected, target was detached elsewhere").
//! The connection corresponding to this client context
+	//! Routing target for SQL execution while CONNECT-ed (CONNECT/DISCONNECT). When is_connected is
+	//! true and connected_to_database can be locked, the chokepoint dispatches non-control SQL via
+	//! `Catalog::RemoteExecute(string)` and wraps the returned TableRef into a SelectStatement.
+	// Pre-flight: IsRemoteCatalog() is the capability declaration; catalogs that return true MUST
+	// implement RemoteExecute(string). Validation runs before mutation so a throw leaves the client
+		throw InvalidInputException("Database \"%s\" does not support CONNECT", target->GetName());
+//! True if `type` is a CONNECT control statement that must execute against LOCAL even while a
+//! CONNECT binding is active (i.e. the chokepoint must let it fall through, not rewrite it).
+//! Wrap a TableRef returned from Catalog::RemoteExecute into `SELECT * FROM <ref>` for the chokepoint.
+	// CONNECT chokepoint: when connected, non-control SQL is rewritten in place and falls through to
+	// the normal pipeline. No recursion — the rewrite goes through PendingStatementInternal, not back here.
+				// Prepared-statement execution path (statement is null, prepared is set). Parameterized
+				// prepared statements would need parameter substitution we don't do in v0 — reject.
+				// No-param prepared statements have a fully-resolved `query` already, route them.
+					        "Parameterized prepared statements cannot be executed while CONNECT-ed; "
+					        "DISCONNECT first, or run the SQL as a fresh statement to route through "
+				// Clear prepared so the rest of the function takes the fresh-statement path on the
+				// Target was detached elsewhere; user must explicitly DISCONNECT to clear is_connected.
+				        "The connected database has been detached out from under this connection. Issue "
+				        "DISCONNECT to clear the connection before running further SQL.")),
+			// Dispatch via the catalog — IsRemoteCatalog() was validated at CONNECT time, so RemoteExecute
+			// is contracted to be implemented. Wrap the returned TableRef into a SelectStatement.
+			// statement is now SELECT * FROM <remote-ref>; fall through.
-# description: All grammar forms of CONNECT / DISCONNECT parse but are not yet implemented.
-# CONNECT has 4 forms (bare / LOCAL / identifier / string literal); each binds to LogicalSimple
-# with the appropriate ConnectInfo state and throws a per-form NotImplementedException.
-# Each form is a distinct slot — implementations swap in one at a time as follow-up PRs land
-# (and some forms, like DISCONNECT LOCAL, will eventually replace the NotImplemented with a
-# DISCONNECT takes no target — there is only one form, `DISCONNECT;`.

+# description: End-to-end CONNECT pass-through against an in-process quack backend.
+# Spin up an in-process quack server we can connect to.
+# Trivial SELECT — chokepoint rewrites as `SELECT * FROM <fn>('qk', 'SELECT 42')` and the
+# normal pipeline runs it; the table function ships to the quack server.
+# A query that returns multiple rows / columns. Stream comes back through the GetConnectFunction
+SELECT range AS x, range * 2 AS y FROM range(3);
+# CONNECT while already bound — single-binding rule errors with DISCONNECT hint.
+# After DISCONNECT, the next SELECT goes through the normal LOCAL pipeline.
+# We can CONNECT again after DISCONNECT.
+# DOCUMENTATION: backend-state persistence across DISCONNECT / re-CONNECT
+# Current implementation: DISCONNECT only flips ClientContext::bound_database;
+# it does NOT close any underlying backend session. The QuackClientConnection
+# lives on the AttachedDatabase from ATTACH until DETACH. So any state the
+# backend was holding (open transactions, session variables, prepared
+# statements, temp tables) persists across DISCONNECT / re-CONNECT cycles.
+# In-process quack note: because quack_serve runs the server in-process and
+# shares this DuckDB's catalog, table state created via pass-through is also
+# visible from LOCAL — that's a quack-in-process artifact rather than CONNECT
+# semantics. The assertions below avoid relying on shared-catalog behavior
+# and focus on the round-trip itself.
+# Open design question: should DISCONNECT close the backend session, leaving
+# re-CONNECT to open a fresh one? See memory/project_connect_roadmap.md.
+# Set a session variable on the backend, DISCONNECT, re-CONNECT, read it back.
+# Today the variable IS still there (we did not close the backend session).
+# Scenario: local transaction state and backend state are independent.
+#   BEGIN;                  -- shipped to qk; backend tx starts on qk's session
+#   SET VARIABLE v = 7;     -- shipped to qk; backend session var
+#   ROLLBACK;               -- LOCAL rollback (only the LOCAL tx is rolled back)
+#   CONNECT qk;             -- re-bind; same backend session
+#   getvariable('v')        -- shipped to qk; still 7 (backend tx still open!)
+# This documents that LOCAL and backend transaction state do not coordinate.
+# Whether that's the desired contract is the open semantics question.
+# description: Current expected behavior for all 4 CONNECT + 1 DISCONNECT grammar forms.
+# CONNECT; — bare form, not yet implemented.
+Not implemented Error: CONNECT with no target is not yet implemented
+# CONNECT LOCAL; — keyword form, not yet implemented.
+Not implemented Error: CONNECT LOCAL is not yet implemented
+# CONNECT <name>; — identifier form, IMPLEMENTED. Errors now come from the dispatch logic, not
+# Attached but unsupported backend → HasConnectFunction() gate fires.
+# CONNECT '<connection_string>'; — string-literal form, not yet implemented.
+Not implemented Error: CONNECT '<connection_string>' is not yet implemented
+# DISCONNECT; — bare form, IMPLEMENTED. Errors when there is no active connection.
